commit.message:
Document go vet unsafe.Pointer false positive

The go vet warning in internal/x11/shm/shm.go is a known false positive
that cannot be suppressed. The code follows unsafe.Pointer rule (6) for
syscall conversions and is identical to patterns in golang.org/x/sys/unix.

Changes:
- Enhanced documentation in shmAttach() explaining why the conversion is safe
- Marked AUDIT.md HIGH finding as resolved with detailed explanation
- Documented that golangci-lint (with unsafeptr disabled) is the correct
  linting tool for production use, not raw go vet
- Referenced existing VET.md documentation

The callback-demo issue mentioned in the same finding was already fixed
in the CRITICAL findings section.

commit.message:
Add automate-this plugin: screen recording to automation scripts (#930)

* Add automate-this plugin: screen recording to automation scripts

New plugin that analyzes screen recordings of manual processes and
proposes working automation scripts at multiple complexity tiers.

Features:
- Frame extraction via ffmpeg for visual process reconstruction
- Optional audio transcription via Whisper for narrated recordings
- Environment fingerprinting to constrain proposals to installed tools
- Application-specific automation strategies (browser, spreadsheet,
  email, terminal, file management, macOS-native)
- Three-tier proposal system (quick win, full script, scheduled automation)
- Dry-run support for safe testing of all proposals

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update skills/automate-this/SKILL.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback on automate-this plugin

- Fix privacy section: clarify that frames/audio are processed locally
  but sent to the Copilot model for analysis (not purely local)
- Replace 'which' with 'command -v' for reliable tool detection with
  explicit NO_FFMPEG/NO_WHISPER sentinels
- Use per-run secure temp directory (mktemp -d, mode 0700) instead of
  fixed /tmp/ paths to prevent other users reading extracted frames
- Add -y flag and -loglevel warning to ffmpeg calls to prevent
  overwrite prompts and avoid piping hiding exit codes
- Add whisper-cpp CLI invocation path so users who install only
  whisper-cpp get working transcription
- Fix grouped command syntax in environment fingerprint to prevent
  false 'not installed' fallthrough

commit.message:
Add RST_STREAM frame type for cancellation propagation (#40)

* Initial plan

* Add RST_STREAM frame type and cancellation support across Rust, Go, and Node

- Add RST_STREAM (kind=3) frame constant to all three languages
- Add rst_stream/rstStreamPacket packet constructors
- Rust: CancellationToken on Request, cancel on inbound RST_STREAM, send RST_STREAM on timeout
- Go: context.Context on Request, cancel on inbound RST_STREAM, send RST_STREAM on timeout
- Node: send RST_STREAM on timeout, handle outbound RST_STREAM

Co-authored-by: bytemain <13938334+bytemain@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add RST_STREAM tests for Rust, Go, and Node

- Rust: test cancel propagation via CancellationToken, outbound RST_STREAM resolution, packet roundtrip
- Go: test cancel propagation via context.Context, packet roundtrip
- Node: test rstStreamPacket helper and RST_STREAM encode/decode roundtrip
- Fix: use separate cancel token/function maps to persist across TRAILERS removal

Co-authored-by: bytemain <13938334+bytemain@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add stream-level cancel support: BidiStream.cancel() in Rust/Go, RST_STREAM handling in Node server

- Rust: BidiStream::cancel() sends RST_STREAM and removes pending call
- Go: BidiStream.Cancel() sends RST_STREAM and removes pending call
- Node server: handlePacket handles RST_STREAM to clean up stream state
- Tests: stream cancel propagation tests in Rust and Go

Co-authored-by: bytemain <13938334+bytemain@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add AbortSignal to Node server handlers for cancellation detection

Server handlers now receive an AbortSignal (3rd parameter) that is
aborted when the peer sends RST_STREAM. This mirrors Rust's
CancellationToken and Go's context.Context for handler-side
cancellation awareness.

Handler example:
  server.register('Method', async (payload, metadata, signal) => {
    signal.addEventListener('abort', () => { /* cleanup */ })
    // ... do work, check signal.aborted ...
  })

Co-authored-by: bytemain <13938334+bytemain@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add descriptive reason to AbortSignal.abort() on RST_STREAM

Co-authored-by: bytemain <13938334+bytemain@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
PHP 8.4: Replace deprecated filter_input() patterns with WordPress-native equivalents (#1639)

* Replace filter_input() without filter types with WordPress-native patterns

Replace all filter_input() calls that have no explicit filter type argument
in wordcamp-reports report classes and wordcamp-speaker-feedback admin.php
with WordPress-native equivalents:

- filter_input( INPUT_POST/GET, 'action' ) → sanitize_text_field( wp_unslash( $_POST/GET['action'] ?? '' ) )
- filter_input( INPUT_POST, self::$slug . '-nonce' ) → wp_unslash( $_POST[ self::$slug . '-nonce' ] ?? '' )
- filter_input( INPUT_POST/GET, 'start-date'/'end-date' ) → sanitize_text_field( wp_unslash( ... ) )
- filter_input( INPUT_POST/GET, 'wordcamp-id' ) → absint( $_POST/GET['wordcamp-id'] ?? 0 )
- filter_input( INPUT_GET, 'period'/'status'/'report' ) → sanitize_text_field( wp_unslash( ... ) )
- sanitize_text_field( filter_input( INPUT_POST, 'search-query' ) ) → sanitize_text_field( wp_unslash( ... ) )
- Nonces use wp_unslash() only (no sanitize_text_field)
- Integer IDs use absint()
- filter_input() calls with explicit FILTER_* constants are left unchanged

* Replace filter_input() without filter types with WordPress-native patterns
* Reports: Remove redundant sanitize_text_field from comparison-only superglobal vars

Remove the sanitize_text_field() wrapper from variables that are only
used for wp-admin comparisons (e.g., against 'Export CSV', 'Run', status
strings) and are never stored to the database or sent to external APIs.

wp_unslash() alone is sufficient for these comparison-only variables.
The affected variables are: $action, $period, $status, $start_date,
$end_date, $search_query, $comment_status, $requested_status,
$helpful, and $search.

* Remove unnecessary sanitize_text_field() from wp-admin comparison-only variables
* phpcs: Disable NonceVerification sniff globally in phpcs.xml.dist

commit.message:
Add USB iPod Gadget support for car stereo adapters

Adds documentation and deployment instructions for using the Pi as a USB
iPod gadget via dwc2 peripheral mode + ipod-gadget kernel modules. This
approach enables compatibility with car stereos that communicate over USB
(30-pin pins 23/25) rather than serial.

Requires physical USB data path from 30-pin connector to Pi test pads
PP22/PP23 (D+/D-). See USB Connectivity section in README.

Key components:
- ipod-gadget kernel modules (g_ipod_hid, g_ipod_audio, g_ipod_gadget)
- oandrew/ipod Go client cross-compiled for ARMv6
- dwc2 overlay in peripheral mode under [all] section
- ipod-gadget.service systemd unit
- iPodUSB ALSA card for digital audio over USB (44.1kHz/16-bit)

commit.message:
Fix Dart float compatibility in JSON output

JavaScript's JSON.stringify drops .0 from integer-valued floats (e.g.
128.0 → 128), but Dart's JSON parser returns int for 128, causing type
cast errors when Flutter expects double values.

The serializer now uses the widget registry's property type metadata to
ensure 'number' type fields always include .0 (128.0) while 'integer'
and 'color' type fields stay as ints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add safe push workflow for PR review workers (#288)

## Problem

PR review workers were pushing to the wrong remote (e.g., pushing to
`origin/PureWeen` when the PR comes from a fork like `vitek-karas`), and
using `--force-with-lease` after rebasing unnecessarily.

### Root Cause (verified by testing)

Workers were using manual checkout:
```bash
git fetch origin pull/<N>/head:pr-<N>
git checkout pr-<N>
```

This creates a branch with **no tracking info** (`Remote: NONE`, `Merge:
NONE`). When the worker then runs `git push` or `git push origin`, git
defaults to `origin` (PureWeen/PolyPilot) — even if the PR came from a
fork.

In contrast, `gh pr checkout <N>` correctly sets up tracking:
- Same-repo PR #247: `Remote: origin`, `Merge:
refs/heads/copilot/build-notification-and-timers` ✅
- Fork PR #280: `Remote: vitek-karas`, `Merge:
refs/heads/Add-a-way-to-specify-polypilot-clone-root-7299` ✅

The second problem was `git rebase origin/main` + `--force-with-lease`,
which requires force pushing. Using `git merge origin/main` adds a merge
commit instead — no force push needed.

## Changes

### `push-to-pr.sh`
A shell script that:
1. Reads PR metadata via `gh pr view` to find the correct owner and
branch
2. Maps the owner to the correct git remote
3. Pushes to that remote **without** `--force`
4. Verifies the push landed by comparing local and remote HEADs
5. Refuses to push if current branch doesn't match the PR branch (guards
against wrong-branch pushes)

### `.squad/routing.md`
Updated Fix Process replacing rebase+force-push with merge+safe-push,
with clear explanation of why `gh pr checkout` is required.

### `.squad/decisions.md`
Explicit shared rules:
- Never force push
- Always use `gh pr checkout` (not manual fetch)
- Always merge (not rebase)
- Always verify push target before pushing
- Use `push-to-pr.sh` for all PR pushes

### `.squad/team.md`
Team definition file (required for squad directory to be recognized by
PolyPilot's squad discovery).

Fix auto-login failure when DATABASE_URL uses default port 3306 (#36)

* Initial plan

* Fix auto-login by casting port to int before comparison

Cast port to int before strict comparison to handle type coercion from rawurldecode.
This ensures the default MySQL port 3306 is correctly detected regardless of type,
preventing session key mismatch between storage and lookup.

commit.message:
MCP Server output contract dual channel revamp (#52)

## Summary

This PR revamps the MCP tool output contract across the server to make
responses more consistent, compact, and machine-friendly, while
preserving LLM-readable text content.

### Key outcomes

- Standardizes tool outputs to compact JSON for consistency and lower
token overhead.
- Introduces dual-channel MCP results for key endpoints:
  - `content` text for model-readable summaries
  - `structuredContent` for reliable programmatic consumption
- Normalizes tool metadata annotations (`title`, `readOnlyHint`,
`destructiveHint`, `idempotentHint`, `openWorldHint`) across the tool
surface.
- Adds shared helper utilities to centralize serialization and
standardized success/error result shaping.
- Adds `.cursor/` and `.claude/` to `.gitignore`.

## What changed

### 1) Shared output helpers (`helpers.py`)

Added reusable utilities:

- `to_serializable(...)` for SDK objects/enums/dates
- `compact_json(...)` for compact JSON serialization
- `build_success_result(...)` for consistent successful `CallToolResult`
- `build_error_result(...)` for consistent error `CallToolResult`

### 2) Server-wide output contract normalization (`server.py`)

- Updated tool implementations to return compact/structured payloads
consistently.
- Applied standardized error envelopes and response formatting patterns.
- Completed annotation hint sets across all MCP tools (44 tool
definitions now include annotations).

### 3) Repo hygiene (`.gitignore`)

- Ignored local IDE/config folders:
  - `.cursor/`
  - `.claude/`

## Why

Before this change, output formatting and metadata varied across tools,
which made downstream parsing and orchestration less predictable. This
PR establishes a single, consistent output contract for both LLM
interactions and programmatic clients.

## Compatibility / behavior notes

- External behavior remains functionally aligned with existing tools.
- Main change is response shape consistency and metadata normalization.
- Error responses are more structured and uniform.

## Commit set included

- `feat(mcp): standardize tool outputs to compact structured JSON with
dual-channel content`
- `Revamp MCP tool output contract and normalize tool annotations`
- `Standardize MCP outputs + tool annotations`
- `chore(mcp): complete tool annotation hint sets`

---------
